skill.name: c4-component
skill.description: Creates C4 Component-level architecture documentation from code-level notes. It defines component boundaries, interfaces, dependencies, relationships, and Mermaid component diagrams.
skill.summary: Turn C4 code-level notes into component-level architecture documentation.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, network, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns. Review found only markdown templates, C4 architecture terminology, local documentation references, and one benign documentation link. No prompt injection, executable code, credential access, data exfiltration, or malicious intent was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Detected as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged backticks and fenced markdown examples as Ruby or shell execution. These lines are documentation examples and templates, not executable code paths.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file contains markdown prose and fenced Mermaid or markdown templates. No executable script, interpreter directive, or command invocation is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: C4 and Markdown Terms Detected as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged many lines as weak cryptography. The reviewed lines contain C4 model terminology, markdown links, placeholders, and documentation text, not cryptographic algorithms or APIs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged content does not include MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or cryptographic API calls. The signals are caused by documentation words and file extensions.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Documentation Link Detected as Network Activity
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL points to c4model.com as a cited source for component diagram principles. The skill does not fetch the URL or transmit data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL appears inside markdown reference text only. No network API, command, or instruction to contact the URL is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Architecture Terms Detected as Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged system and component diagram text as reconnaissance. The lines describe C4 architecture documentation concepts, not host discovery or environment inspection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The terms appear in architecture diagram instructions and output expectations. There are no commands such as uname, whoami, ipconfig, or filesystem enumeration.

content.value_statement: Teams often have detailed code notes but lack clear component-level architecture views. This skill turns code-level C4 documentation into component boundaries, interfaces, relationships, and diagrams.
actual_capabilities: Synthesizes multiple C4 code-level files into logical components.
Defines component names, purposes, responsibilities, and boundaries.
Documents interfaces with protocols, operations, and dependency notes.
Creates Mermaid C4Component diagrams for one container.
Builds a master component index linking component documentation.
Explains distinctions between C4 code, component, container, and context levels.
limitations: It depends on accurate code-level documentation or source context.
It does not execute architecture discovery tools or inspect runtime systems.
It may need human review for ambiguous component boundaries.
It provides templates and guidance, not a full architecture governance process.

use_cases.title: Prepare Architecture Reviews
use_cases.description: Convert code-level notes into component diagrams and interface summaries before a review.

use_cases.title: Document Service Internals
use_cases.description: Map modules, adapters, databases, and external dependencies within one service container.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Documentation
use_cases.description: Create consistent component pages and a master index across a codebase.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Component Page
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the c4-component skill to create a component-level document for this service. Include purpose, features, code elements, interfaces, and dependencies.

prompt_templates.title: Synthesize Code-Level Notes
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the c4-component skill to synthesize these c4-code files into logical components. Explain each boundary decision and list included files.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Component Diagram
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the c4-component skill to create a Mermaid C4Component diagram for this container. Show internal components and external dependencies.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Component Index
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the c4-component skill to produce a master component index for this subsystem. Include relationships, documentation links, and review notes.

output_examples.input: A set of c4-code notes for authentication controllers, token services, user repositories, and audit logging.
output_examples.output: A component document with authentication boundary rationale, interface summaries, dependencies, and a Mermaid component diagram.

output_examples.input: An API layer with handlers, validation modules, service clients, and persistence adapters.
output: Included code elements for each component.
Relationships between API, validation, client, and persistence components.

output_examples.input: Several component documents that need a system-level index.
output_examples.output: A master component index with links, relationship notes, and a component relationship diagram summary.

best_practices: Provide source files, existing C4 code notes, and known container boundaries.
Ask for boundary rationale when components overlap or share responsibilities.
Review generated diagrams with engineers who know the implementation.
anti_patterns: Do not use it for unrelated architecture levels without adapting the scope.
Do not treat generated boundaries as final without technical review.
Do not omit external dependencies when they affect component behavior.

faq.question: What C4 level does this skill support?
faq.answer: It focuses on the C4 Component level, which describes components inside one container.

faq.question: Can it create Mermaid diagrams?
faq.answer: Yes. It provides Mermaid C4Component diagram guidance and expected relationship structure.

faq.question: Does it analyze source code automatically?
faq.answer: No. It uses the context and documentation supplied in the prompt or workspace.

faq.question: What input works best?
faq.answer: C4 code-level documentation, module descriptions, interface notes, dependency lists, and container scope.

faq.question: Can Claude, Codex, and Claude Code use it?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is this a security-focused skill?
faq.answer: No. It is a documentation skill for software architecture and component diagrams.
